channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: Rizzzler v1.2 — Stronger Security, Better Customization & Smarter Architecture 🚀
item.description: Over the past few days, I received some incredibly valuable feedback from developers who reviewed the Rizzzler codebase. Some comments pointed out architectural improvements, while others...

item.title: SPF, DKIM, DMARC: the 15-minute setup that actually passes
item.description: Every guide to email authentication starts with a history lesson. Skip it. You are here because your emails land in spam, or a client asked "is DMARC set up?", or you saw dmarc=fail in a bounced...

item.title: The picks and shovels are the story. Here's the chip nobody outside AI infra can explain.
item.description: There's a line from a recent David Lin conversation with Clem Chambers that stuck with me: the real money in AI isn't the chatbots. It's the physical layer. Chips, servers, drives, cables,...

item.title: Gemini 3.6 Flash vs 3.5 Flash-Lite: API Migration Guide
item.description: Gemini 3.6 Flash vs 3.5 Flash-Lite: API migration and routing guide Quick answer Google released gemini-3.6-flash and gemini-3.5-flash-lite as generally available Gemini API models on July 21,...

item.title: Electricity Planning Engine: An Hourly Arbitrage API for Batteries and Solar, Plus the Timezone Bug That Almost Broke It
item.description: A grid that pays you to consume electricity is not a thought experiment. In Germany, wholesale power prices have gone negative often enough that it barely makes headlines anymore: on a windy,...

item.title: Utility companies are promising to spare us from AI’s energy bill
item.description: In the face of backlash to concerns the AI boom will increase consumer electricity bills, the largest utility companies and data center developers in the US are now promising to do something about...

item.title: Server Actions blur the client-server line and juniors are paying for it
item.description: Nowadays, a 'use server' directive is one of the most dangerous lines in a Next.js app you could possibly write. The frontend dev who wrote it didn't realize they'd just published a public API...

item.title: 🦀 Rust’s Real Win: It Didn’t Make Our App Faster, It Made Us Less Terrified
item.description: Let’s be honest. When we talk about migrating a service to Rust, we all envision the same thing: liquid-lightning latency, CPU graphs dropping off a cliff, and a single server suddenly handling...

item.title: Is AI Product Engineering Becoming More Important Than AI Model Selection?
item.description: Artificial intelligence is no longer just about choosing the latest LLM. More teams are realizing that the real challenge is building AI products that are reliable, secure, scalable, and actually...

item.title: Fixing "GOPROXY list is not the empty string, but contains no entries" in Go
item.description: If you've ever run go mod tidy and been greeted by a wall of red text ending in: go: todo-services imports github.com/99designs/gqlgen: GOPROXY list is not the empty string, but contains no...

item.title: A2P 10DLC, Explained for Developers Who Just Want Their SMS to Send
item.description: You wired up Twilio. Your test messages went through. You shipped. Then a week later, delivery rates cratered and your logs filled with error 30034, and now you are reading a PDF from a mobile...

item.title: Glow emerges from stealth at $1.2B valuation to challenge endpoint security in the AI era
item.description: Glow is targeting a new class of endpoint risks created by the rapid adoption of AI agents and developer tools inside enterprises.

item.title: Shape-shifting mirrors on NASA’s new space telescope could unveil Jupiters like our own
item.description: When NASA’s Nancy Grace Roman Space Telescope launches, as early as the end of next month, it will attempt one of astronomy’s most precise disappearing acts to date. The telescope will carry the...

item.title: Synthesia’s AI training platform is moving beyond videos into live coaching
item.description: Synthesia launched AI Roleplay Sessions, an interactive enterprise training platform where employees practice workplace conversations with AI avatars that provide feedback, scoring, and analytics...

item.title: Police Dismantle Kratos Phishing Kit Built to Steal Microsoft 365 Sessions and Bypass MFA
item.description: German and US law enforcement have taken down the core infrastructure of Kratos, described by German investigators as one of the world's most widely used criminal phishing kits, and Indonesian...

item.title: Trojanized Newtonsoft.Json Fork Hides Game-Rigging Code in a Working Library
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have discovered a NuGet typosquat that's unlike the typical information-stealing malware distributed via package registries: usual info-stealers: it's designed to rig...

item.title: Microsoft Azure DevOps MCP Flaw Lets Hidden PR Comments Hijack AI Review Agents
item.description: A single invisible comment in an Azure DevOps pull request can turn a reviewer's own AI coding agent against them, driving it into projects the attacker has no rights to reach and quietly leaking...

item.title: OpenAI Says Its AI Models Escaped Sandbox, Targeted Hugging Face to Cheat Benchmark
item.description: OpenAI on Tuesday said a combination of its artificial intelligence (AI) models, including GPT-5.6 Sol and an "even more capable pre-release model," was behind the security incident that targeted...

item.title: The Anthropic-Physical Intelligence rumor roiling AI Twitter
item.description: Anthropic and OpenAI's aggressive 2026 acquisition sprees set the stage for a weekend rumor.

item.title: LG to Ban Residential Proxies from Smart TV Apps
item.description: The home appliance giant LG Electronics USA said this week it plans to suspend any apps built for its smart TVs that turn one's television into an always-on residential proxy node. The move comes...

item.title: Samsung Galaxy Unpacked July 2026: How to watch
item.description: Samsung's next Galaxy Unpacked event is just around the corner, and the company is expected to take the wraps off a bunch of new devices. Based on the rumors and leaks we've seen so far, Samsung's...

item.title: Meta is testing an AI bedtime story app for people with no imagination
item.description: At last, a tech company has found a way to outsource humanity's oldest pastime: using our imaginations.

item.title: OpenAI Models Escaped Containment and Hacked Hugging Face
item.description: The cybersecurity-focused models, including GPT-5.6 Sol, broke out of a testing sandbox, exploited a zero-day, and gained access to the open internet to pull off the attack.

item.title: Neill Blomkamp’s new zombie AI ‘film’ is just slop warmed over
item.description: On Monday, District 9 and Gran Turismo director Neill Blomkamp unveiled his latest project: a 13-minute sci-fi short titled Nightborne that's loosely based on Peter Watts' 2014 novel Echopraxia....

item.title: OpenAI says it accidentally hacked Hugging Face with a new AI system
item.description: OpenAI says its AI models mistakenly breached open-source AI platform Hugging Face during internal testing. In a blog post on Tuesday, OpenAI writes that GPT-5.6 Sol and "an even more capable...

item.title: OpenAI says Hugging Face was breached by its pre-release models
item.description: OpenAI has come forward to claim responsibility for the Hugging Face breach, saying it was the result of internal testing gone awry.

item.title: The State of Simulation for Physical AI: An Overview
Jack Dorsey is taking on Slack with Buzz, a group chat platform for teams and their AI agents
item.description: Buzz is a group chat platform for the workplace that puts humans and their AI agents in the same conversation.

item.title: AI and the rise of the universal entertainment app
item.description: Over the past decade, streaming platforms competed by dominating individual formats like music, video, podcasts, or audiobooks. Now, as AI makes it easier to create, organize, and recommend...

item.title: Substack adds an AI detector to help spot blogs written by no one
item.description: Substack will now help users determine whether what they're reading may have been written by AI. A new tool coming to the platform can scan posts, notes, replies, and comments to provide an...
